mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, FLT3, and FGFR kinases
additional_notes: Active at low-nanomolar levels in FLT3-ITD AML, including TKI-resistant variants; see Blood 2013; PRISM/GDSC.

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, VEGFR, and PDGFR signaling
additional_notes: Inhibits proliferation in FLT3-ITD AML models (MOLM-13, MV4-11); see GDSC/CTRP. Potency generally low-nanomolar to sub-micromolar per text notes.

mechanism: Primarily inhibits VEGFR; also inhibits FLT3 (including resistant mutants)
additional_notes: Reported AML antiproliferative effects; see PRISM. Potency generally low-nanomolar to sub-micromolar for FLT3-directed TKIs per text.

mechanism: Inhibits MET, AXL, and FLT3 (including D835Y mutant)
additional_notes: Reduces viability of FLT3-ITD AML lines; see Oncotarget 2014; PRISM. Potency generally low-nanomolar to sub-micromolar per text.

mechanism: Targets FLT3 to block downstream signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as CEP-701. Active in MOLM-13/MV4-11; see CTRP. Potency generally low-nanomolar to sub-micromolar per text.

mechanism: Inhibits SYK kinase to reduce AML cell proliferation and viability
additional_notes: Active metabolite R406. SYK dependency documented in subsets of AML; see Cancer Cell 2009; PRISM/GDSC.

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases, ABL, and KIT signaling
additional_notes: Antiproliferative effects in AML; activity may extend to FLT3-ITD contexts in combinations; see Leukemia 2007; GDSC/CTRP.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 to block MAPK signaling
additional_notes: Reduces AML proliferation; strongest effects often in combination, but single-agent activity seen in some lines; PRISM/GDSC.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 to suppress MAPK pathway
additional_notes: Also known as AZD6244. AML line activity reported; PRISM/GDSC.
Antiproliferative effects in some AML lines; variable single-agent activity; PRISM/GDSC.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases leading to chromatin-mediated cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Potent cytotoxicity in AML lines (including MOLM-13); strong activity in combinations; PRISM/CTRP. Potency generally nanomolar to low-micromolar per text.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to suppress AML cell growth
additional_notes: AML line growth inhibition reported; PRISM. Potency generally nanomolar to low-micromolar per text.

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, inducing apoptotic cell death
additional_notes: Nanomolar-range cytotoxicity in AML lines; PRISM/GDSC/CTRP.

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, triggering apoptosis
additional_notes: Strong AML cytotoxicity (including MOLM-13), often active when bortezomib-resistant; PRISM/CTRP. Potency generally nanomolar to low-micromolar per text.

mechanism: Blocks mitochondrial protein synthesis, impairing AML cell energetics
additional_notes: Selectively targets AML mitochondrial translation; reduces viability and affects stem/progenitor populations; PRISM/CTRP. Potency generally low-micromolar per text.

mechanism: Inhibits TrxR, inducing oxidative stress–mediated AML cell death
additional_notes: Activity reported in MOLM-13 and other AML lines; PRISM. Potency generally low-micromolar per text.

mechanism: Uncouples mitochondria and modulates Wnt/STAT3 signaling
additional_notes: Antileukemic activity in AML cell lines commonly reported at low-micromolar potency; PRISM.

mechanism: Inhibits ALDH; with copper, forms complexes that modulate NF-κB/proteasome pathways
additional_notes: Copper-complexed disulfiram shows AML cytotoxicity and stem cell targeting in vitro; see Oncotarget 2011; Leukemia 2016; PRISM.
